data.title: KBRA Releases Research – Electric Vehicle Residual Values in Europe: On the Road to Greater Stability?
data.description: A recent report by KBRA analyzes the trends in electric vehicle (EV) residual values in Europe and their impact on auto asset-backed securities (ABS). After a significant repricing due to supply constraints during the pandemic, data now shows a slowdown in the decline of EV residual values. The report also discusses how factors such as EV adoption rates, expansion of charging infrastructure, and changing policies in the EU and UK could affect these trends. This analysis provides insights into the evolving EV market, indicating potential implications for ABS investors.

data.title: InventHelp Inventor Develops Protective Shield for Vehicle Rims (TLS-548)
data.description: In Pittsburgh on March 12, 2026, an inventor from Southfield, Michigan shared a new creation called the WHEEL BONNET that aims to simplify covering vehicle rims during tire polishing. The inventor expressed a desire to protect rims from accidental overspray and streaking with this design. The innovation addresses the need for a straightforward solution in automotive maintenance, potentially impacting drivers and car enthusiasts.

data.title: Robert Half Selected by Newsweek as One of America's Most Charitable Companies 2026
data.description: A global talent solutions and business consulting company, Robert Half (NYSE: RHI), based in Menlo Park, California, and its subsidiary Protiviti, have recently been honored by Newsweek as one of America's Companies Most Charitable 2026. This prestigious recognition, awarded for the first time, highlights their philanthropic efforts and dedication to giving back to the community. The acknowledgment underscores their commitment to social responsibility and making a positive impact.

data.title: CIEE Announces the Rick A. Ruth BridgeUSA Summer Work Travel Scholarship
data.description: In efforts to promote mutual understanding and peace between the United States and global Muslim communities, the Council on International Educational Exchange (CIEE) is launching a cultural exchange program. This initiative was recently unveiled in Portland, Maine on March 12, 2026. The program aims to foster connections and build bridges through cultural exchange activities and educational opportunities.

data.title: In HelloNation, Funeral Service Expert David Romeo Explains Choosing Cremation or Burial & Their Differences
data.description: On March 12, 2026, in Patchogue, New York, a recent HelloNation article provides helpful advice on how families can make the decision between cremation or burial when a loved one passes away. David Romeo, a professional at Ruland Funeral Home in Patchogue, NY, offers valuable insights in the article. The piece aims to aid families in understanding the different aspects to consider when deciding between the two options for laying their loved ones to rest.

data.title: TrueCoat Painters - Highlands Ranch Community Partner - Exterior House Painting
data.description: In Highlands Ranch, Colorado, a picturesque community, TrueCoat Painters has partnered with the Highlands Ranch Community Association. This partnership aims to enhance the appeal of homes in the area by providing high-quality painting services. The collaboration, announced on March 12, 2026, reflects the importance of both aesthetics and structural integrity in the community.

data.title: The "Brain" Enters the Water: iGarden X Series Debuts as the World’s First AI-Empowered Swim Jet
data.description: San Francisco - The iGarden Swim Jet X Series is announced as the world’s inaugural AI-powered portable swim jet. Revealed on Kickstarter on March 12th at 9:00 AM PDT, this innovation signals a significant change in applying high-output propulsion to residential settings. During the launch event, a special giveaway was announced for the first 48 hours: One fortunate backer who pledges within this timeframe will be chosen at random to receive a complimentary X Series unit from their initial order.

data.title: Union Members Ratify Collective Bargaining Agreement at HII’s Ingalls Shipbuilding
data.description: In Pascagoula, Mississippi, on March 12, 2026, HII disclosed that all five collective bargaining units at its Ingalls Shipbuilding division have approved new collective bargaining agreements. The agreements guarantee an immediate base wage surge of 18 percent or more for union-represented shipbuilders. This wage increase is the most significant in Ingalls Shipbuilding history. The newly ratified agreements will prolong the contract until March 8, 2031.
